title: drepananthus biovulatus
intro: Drepananthus biovulatus, also known as the Bintangor tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Drepananthus biovulatus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource for herbal medicine. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion by steeping its roots or leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting its active compounds with a solvent.
